Interface SourceInfo

All Superinterfaces:
ProgramModelInfo, Service
All Known Subinterfaces:
CrossReferenceSourceInfo
All Known Implementing Classes:
DefaultCrossReferenceSourceInfo, DefaultSourceInfo, KeYCrossReferenceSourceInfo, SchemaCrossReferenceSourceInfo
public interface SourceInfo extends ProgramModelInfo
Implements queries for program model elements with concrete syntactical representations.

  • Field Summary

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    static final Statement
    METHOD_EXIT
    This is a pseudo-statement representing the exit of a method as returned by getSucceedingStatements(Statement).

    Adds a progress listener for the model update process.

    Returns the (annotation) type of the given annotation use.

    Returns the declared constructor.

    Returns the referred constructor, if there is an unambiguous one.

    Returns the locally defined constructors of the given type declaration.

    Returns all constructors that are applicable with respect to the given reference.

    Returns the syntactical counterpart of the given method.

  • Field Details

    • METHOD_EXIT

      static final Statement METHOD_EXIT
      This is a pseudo-statement representing the exit of a method as returned by getSucceedingStatements(Statement).
      Since:
      0.71

  • Method Details

    • getContainingClassType

      ClassType getContainingClassType(ProgramElement context)
      Returns the class type that contains the given program element.
      Parameters:
      context - a program element.
      Returns:
      the type to which the given program element belongs (may be null).

    • getPackage

      Package getPackage(PackageReference r)
      Returns the referred package.
      Parameters:
      r - a package reference.
      Returns:
      the referred package (may be null).

    • getType

      Type getType(ProgramElement pe)
      Returns the type for the given program element.
      Parameters:
      pe - the program element to compute the type for.
      Returns:
      the type for that object (may be null).

    • getType

      Type getType(TypeReference tr)
      Returns the referred type.
      Parameters:
      tr - a type reference.
      Returns:
      the referred type (may be null).

    • getType

      ClassType getType(TypeDeclaration td)
      Returns the declared type.
      Parameters:
      td - a type declaration.
      Returns:
      the corresponding type.

    • getType

      Type getType(VariableSpecification vs)
      Returns the type for the variable specification. Implementors should ensure that, if vs is an EnumConstantSpecification, the call is delegated to getType(VariableDefinition).
      Parameters:
      vs - a variable specification.
      Returns:
      the type for that variable (may be null).

    • getType

      Type getType(String name, ProgramElement context)
      Tries to find a type with the given name using the given program element as context. Useful to check for name clashes when introducing a new identifier. Neither name nor context may be null.
      Parameters:
      name - the name for the type to be looked up; may or may not be qualified.
      context - a program element defining the lookup context (scope).
      Returns:
      the corresponding type (may be null).

    • getType

      Type getType(Expression expr)
      Returns the type of the given expression (may be null).
      Parameters:
      expr - an expression.
      Returns:
      the type of the expression, or null if the type could not be computed.

    • isNarrowingTo

      boolean isNarrowingTo(Expression expr, PrimitiveType to)
      Checks automatic narrowing from compile-time constant ints to byte, char, or short. Returns false if the primitive type is not one of byte, char, short, or if the expression is not a compile-time constant int as defined in the Java language specification.
      Parameters:
      expr - the expression that might be a compile-time constant fitting into the given type.
      to - the type that the expression might fit into.
      Returns:
      true if the expression value is "constant" and would fit into a variable of the given type without loss of information, false in any other case.

    • getVariable

      Variable getVariable(String name, ProgramElement context)
      Tries to find a variable with the given name using the given program element as context. The variable may be a local variable, a parameter or a field. Useful to check for name clashes when introducing a new identifier.
      Parameters:
      name - the name of the variable to be looked up.
      context - a program element defining the lookup context.
      Returns:
      the corresponding variable (may be null).

    • getVariable

      Variable getVariable(VariableSpecification vs)
      Returns the declared variable.
      Parameters:
      vs - a variable definition.
      Returns:
      the corresponding variable.

    • getVariable

      Variable getVariable(VariableReference vr)
      Returns the referred variable.
      Parameters:
      vr - a variable reference.
      Returns:
      the referred variable (may be null).

    • getField

      Field getField(FieldReference fr)
      Returns the referred field.
      Parameters:
      fr - a field reference.
      Returns:
      the referred field (may be null).

    • getFields

      List<FieldSpecification> getFields(TypeDeclaration td)
      Returns the locally defined fields of the given type declaration. This method does not report inherited fields. The returned list matches the syntactic order of the declarations.
      Parameters:
      td - a type declaration.
      Returns:
      a list of fields that are members of the declaration.
      See Also:
      • ProgramModelInfo.getFields(ClassType)
      • ProgramModelInfo.getAllFields(ClassType)

    • getTypes

      List<TypeDeclaration> getTypes(TypeDeclaration td)
      Returns the locally defined inner types of the given type declaration. This method does not report inherited types. The returned list matches the syntactic order of the declarations.
      Parameters:
      td - a type declaration.
      Returns:
      a list of inner types that are members of the declaration.
      See Also:
      • ProgramModelInfo.getTypes(ClassTypeContainer)
      • ProgramModelInfo.getAllTypes(ClassType)

    • getMethod

      Method getMethod(MethodDeclaration md)
      Returns the declared method.
      Parameters:
      md - a method declaration.
      Returns:
      the corresponding method.

    • getMethods

      List<Method> getMethods(TypeDeclaration td)
      Returns the locally defined methods of the given type declaration. This method does not report inherited methods. The returned list matches the syntactic order of the declarations. If td is an enum declaration, implicit members are also added.
      Parameters:
      td - a type declaration.
      Returns:
      a list of methods that are members of the declaration.
      See Also:
      • ProgramModelInfo.getMethods(ClassType)
      • ProgramModelInfo.getAllMethods(ClassType)

    • getMethod

      Method getMethod(MethodReference mr)
      Returns the referred method, if there is an unambiguous one.
      Parameters:
      mr - the method reference to collate.
      Returns:
      the referred method.
      Throws:
      AmbiguousReferenceException - if there are no single most specific applicable method.
      UnresolvedReferenceException - if there are no applicable method.
      See Also:
      • getMethods(MethodReference)

    • getAnnotationProperty

      AnnotationProperty getAnnotationProperty(AnnotationPropertyReference apr)

    • getMethods

      List<Method> getMethods(MethodReference mr)
      Returns all methods that are applicable with respect to the given reference. Only the most specific methods are returned; the program is not semantically correct if there is more than one such method.
      Parameters:
      mr - the method reference to collate.
      Returns:
      the list of applicable methods.
      See Also:
      • getMethod(MethodReference)

    • getConstructor

      Constructor getConstructor(ConstructorDeclaration cd)
      Returns the declared constructor.
      Parameters:
      cd - a constructor declaration.
      Returns:
      the corresponding constructor.

    • getConstructors

      List<Constructor> getConstructors(TypeDeclaration td)
      Returns the locally defined constructors of the given type declaration. The returned list matches the syntactic order of the declarations. This method always returns a non-empty list, at least containing a DefaultConstructor.
      Parameters:
      td - a type declaration.
      Returns:
      a list of constructors that are members of the declaration.
      See Also:
      • ProgramModelInfo.getConstructors(ClassType)

    • getConstructor

      Constructor getConstructor(ConstructorReference cr)
      Returns the referred constructor, if there is an unambiguous one.
      Parameters:
      cr - the constructor reference to collate.
      Returns:
      the constructor referred.
      Throws:
      AmbiguousReferenceException - if there are is no most specific applicable constructor.
      UnresolvedReferenceException - if there are no applicable constructor.
      See Also:
      • getConstructors(ConstructorReference)

    • getConstructors

      List<? extends Constructor> getConstructors(ConstructorReference cr)
      Returns all constructors that are applicable with respect to the given reference. Only the most specific constructors are returned; the program is not semantically correct if there is more than one such constructor.
      Parameters:
      cr - the constructor reference to collate.
      Returns:
      the list of applicable constructors.
      See Also:
      • getConstructor(ConstructorReference)

    • makeSignature

      List<Type> makeSignature(List<Expression> args)
      Creates a signature by resolving the types of the given expression list. An empty or null expression list results in an empty type list.
      Parameters:
      args - a list of expressions (may be null).
      Returns:
      a list of types that correspond to the respective expressions.

    • resolveURQ

      Reference resolveURQ(UncollatedReferenceQualifier urq)
      Replaces the uncollated qualifier by its proper counterpart. Returns null, if this was not possible, the replaced reference otherwise. Note that this method does not notify the change history of the change!
      Parameters:
      urq - an uncollated reference.
      Returns:
      a resolved reference, or null if the reference could not be resolved.

    • register

      @Deprecated void register(ProgramElement pe)
      Deprecated.
      this method will not be public in future - use ChangeHistory.attach instead
      Check in a program element and built up all scopes.
      Parameters:
      pe - the root of a syntax tree that shall be analyzed.

    • getTypeDeclaration

      TypeDeclaration getTypeDeclaration(ClassType ct)
      Returns the syntactical counterpart of the given classtype. Returns null, if the given type is not a type declaration.
      Parameters:
      ct - a class type.
      Returns:
      the corresponding type declaration, or null, if the given type has no syntactical representation.

    • getMethodDeclaration

      MethodDeclaration getMethodDeclaration(Method m)
      Returns the syntactical counterpart of the given method. Returns null, if the given method is not a method declaration.
      Parameters:
      m - a method.
      Returns:
      the corresponding method declaration, or null, if the given method has no syntactical representation.

    • getConstructorDeclaration

      ConstructorDeclaration getConstructorDeclaration(Constructor c)
      Returns the syntactical counterpart of the given method. Returns null, if the given method is not a method declaration.
      Parameters:
      c - a constructor.
      Returns:
      the corresponding method declaration, or null, if the given method has no syntactical representation.

    • getVariableSpecification

      VariableSpecification getVariableSpecification(Variable v)
      Returns the syntactical counterpart of the given variable. Returns null, if the given variable is not a variable specification.
      Parameters:
      v - a variable.
      Returns:
      the corresponding variable specification, or null, if the given variable has no syntactical representation.

    • getSucceedingStatements

      List<Statement> getSucceedingStatements(Statement s)
      Returns this list of statements that will possibly be executed after the specified statement. This method defines the edges of a control flow graph and obeys the Java compile-time definitions for reachability. The graph is not transitive: The last statement of a loop will return the loop as successor only, and the loop may have a successor that actually leaves the loop. Statement that can leave the top level statement block will report METHOD_EXITas a successor.
      Parameters:
      s - a statement.
      Returns:
      a list of succeeding statements.
      Since:
      0.71

    • addProgressListener

      void addProgressListener(ProgressListener l)
      Adds a progress listener for the model update process.
      Since:
      0.72

    • removeProgressListener

      void removeProgressListener(ProgressListener l)
      Removes a progress listener for the model update process.
      Since:
      0.72

    • getAnnotationType

      Type getAnnotationType(AnnotationUseSpecification au)
      Returns the (annotation) type of the given annotation use.
      Parameters:
      au - an annotation use
      Returns:
      the type of the referenced annotation type
